title: Active vs installed boot payload SDMMC window compare
question: Does the newer installed U-Boot/boot package contain changed SDMMC/MBUS/NSI/security windows that justify testing a bootloader update as an IDMAC fix?
window_compare: sdmmc0_mod early: 0x400-byte window identical; 0x1000-byte window 99.30% identical.
sdc_mbus table: 0x400-byte window identical; 0x1000-byte window 71.94% identical due to nearby non-anchor changes.
security_state window changed mostly in BL31 build string/version: active 2025-02-26 5fc237a6a vs installed 2025-07-02 48e54578a.
ARISC SMC clock-glitch window is 0x1000-byte identical.
decision: No bootloader restage and no kernel patch. The installed package does not expose a changed SDMMC/MBUS/ARISC window that plausibly explains the Linux SDMMC0 IDMAC descriptor-fetch stall.
next_action: Do not spend a Cubie bootloader-update cycle on this evidence alone. Continue looking for a named SDMMC0 access-control handoff or maintainer/vendor feedback.
